primary_question: Does PGGT1B reproduce a stimulated CD4+ program shift under Stim8hr after orthogonal knockdown?
promote_if: PGGT1B orthogonal knockdown reproduces a stimulated program shift in Stim8hr with activation-marker flow cytometry and targeted RNA-seq, without a matched Rest-only or broad non-immune signal.
weaken_if: Rest-only shift, weak stimulated marker movement, inconsistent targeted RNA-seq direction, or a broad transfer signal stronger than the CD4+ Stim8hr footprint.
reject_if: failed on-target knockdown, no stimulated activation-program shift after knockdown, or a broad non-immune effect that explains the signal better than CD4+ activation gating.
acceptance_gate: replay follow-up evidence through frozen checks, then require human signature
missing_evidence_before_acceptance: orthogonal knockdown; matched donor replicate; activation-marker flow cytometry; targeted RNA-seq at 8h and 48h; human signature over replayed follow-up evidence
